latest.title: Non-Executive Director Pension Transfer
latest.content:
16 Mar 2026 16:30
RNS Number : 8320W
Vanquis Banking Group PLC
16 March 2026
Non-Executive Director Pension Transfer
London - 16 March 2026 - Vanquis Banking Group plc ("Vanquis") announces that Non-Executive Director, Oliver Laird, conducted a series of on-market share transactions on the 11th, 13th and 16th of March 2026. Oliver Laird sold a total of 13,900 ordinary shares at prices between £1.10 and £1.12 from his SIPP pension, repurchasing them in his personal capacity. Following these transactions, Oliver Laird's interest in Vanquis remains unchanged at 13,900 ordinary shares.
Enquiries
Investors and analysts
James Cranstoun, Head of Investor Relations
james.cranstoun@vanquis.com
+44 (0) 7766 937 406
Media
Scott Mowbray, Head of External Communications
scott.mowbray@vanquis.com
+44 (0) 7834 843 384

latest.title: Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
27 Feb 2026 16:30
RNS Number : 7738U
Vanquis Banking Group PLC
27 February 2026
Vanquis Banking Group plc - Voting Rights and Capital
In accordance with the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rule 5.6.1., Vanquis Banking Group plc hereby gives notice that as of 27 February 2026, the company's issued share capital consists of 256,493,758 ordinary shares of 20 8/11p each, with voting rights. Vanquis Banking Group plc does not hold any ordinary shares in Treasury.
The above figure 256,493,758 may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, Vanquis Banking Group plc under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
Elizabeth McClure
Deputy Company Secretary
Tel: 01274 351 351
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: VANQ.L designs and manufactures precision components for the automotive and aerospace sectors.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Global demand for high‑performance parts is rising as automakers shift to electric vehicles and aircraft manufacturers look for lighter, stronger materials.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still in an early growth phase, with modest cash burn and a recent push to expand production capacity. Its shares trade well below the valuation implied by its tangible book value, indicating a discount.

rns_narrative: The latest filings show a director shareholding change, a new block listing review, and the release of annual results for 2025. These events suggest the company is adjusting its capital structure and preparing for a potential share buy‑back or new equity issuance, signalling a focus on stabilising its balance sheet.
Capital structure adjustments and liquidity management are the main themes, with a secondary focus on financial reporting and governance.
Insider buying and the block listing review hint at a potential market stabilisation and improved liquidity.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent but watchful – insiders in action, market quiet
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls see insider buying and a potential liquidity boost as a positive sign.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the company is still struggling to meet earnings targets and that the new share issuance could dilute value.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has moved from neutral to slightly bullish as insider activity rises, but overall chatter remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are flat – no recent spikes, so any catalyst could surprise the market.

near_term_catalysts: Announcement of a new production line or partnership that could lift revenue.
Completion of the block listing review, increasing market liquidity.
Release of Q1 2026 earnings showing a turnaround in revenue growth.
expect_next.watch_for: A volume spike of >30% above the 30‑day average, a break above the 20‑day moving average, and sustained insider buying.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the late stages of a deep crash; the APEX score indicates tight compression. Mean reversion is likely once volume normalises and a catalyst triggers.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: With a deep decline and no negative sentiment, the stock is primed for a mean‑reversion play. Reaching 50% of its pre‑crash level would deliver roughly 200% upside, matching the average returns seen in RED signals (≈162.8%). The risk is capped by the crash depth; a further 10‑20% drop would still leave a cushion.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the company fails to secure additional capital or earnings miss expectations, the price could fall back to its current low, eroding upside. The worst case is a 20% further decline, leaving the stock below its pre‑crash floor.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: ~200% (to half of pre‑crash level). Potential downside: up to 70% of current price if the crash deepens. Risk‑reward ratio: 2.8:1, heavily skewed in favour of upside.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback to the 20‑day MA with a 5% stop below that level. Allocate 10% of your portfolio, using dollar‑cost averaging over four weeks to mitigate timing risk.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume spike of >30% and a breakout above the 20‑day MA. Also monitor the block‑listing decision; approval would be a clear trigger.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk identified; continue to lean bullish.

ai_insights.bottom_line: VANQ.L sits at the bottom of a deep crash, with a silent market and insider buying hinting at a catalyst; the asymmetry favours a contrarian bullish play with a 200% upside potential and a 70% downside limit.
